info.description: Automated Kubernetes deployment updates. Keel is a tool for automating Kubernetes deployment updates.
info.title: Keel API

get.description: Returns 200 OK if the service is healthy
Returns a list of all approvals (both active and archived)

put.description: Sets or removes approval requirements for a resource

post.description: Performs an action on an approval (approve, reject, delete, or archive)
post.summary: Approve, reject, delete, or archive an approval

get.description: Returns paginated audit logs with optional filtering

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of resource kinds to filter
parameters.name: filter

get.description: Returns information about the currently authenticated user

post.description: Authenticates a user with username and password

get.description: Logs out the currently authenticated user

post.description: Logs out the currently authenticated user

get.description: Generates a new authentication token for the current user
Returns information about the currently authenticated user

put.description: Updates the Keel policy for a specific resource

get.description: Returns a list of all tracked Kubernetes resources with their policies
Returns aggregated statistics about webhooks, approvals, and updates
Returns a list of all tracked container images with their triggers and policies

put.description: Updates the trigger type and poll schedule for a tracked resource

post.description: Receives and processes Azure Container Registry webhook notifications for image push events
Receives and processes DockerHub webhook notifications for image push events
Receives and processes GitHub webhook notifications for container registry events (both GitHub Packages and GitHub Container Registry)
Receives and processes Harbor registry webhook notifications for image push events
Receives and processes JFrog Artifactory webhook notifications for image push events
Triggers a deployment update event directly with repository and tag information

parameters.description: Repository information with name and tag
parameters.name: payload

post.description: Receives and processes Quay.io webhook notifications for image push events
Receives and processes Docker Registry V2 notification webhooks for image push events. Compatible with Docker Registry, GitLab Container Registry, and Harbor.

get.description: Returns the current version information of Keel

availableReplicas.description:
Total number of available pods (ready for at least minReadySeconds) targeted by this deployment.
+optional

readyReplicas.description:
Total number of ready pods targeted by this deployment.
+optional

replicas.description:
Total number of non-terminated pods targeted by this deployment (their labels match the selector).
+optional

unavailableReplica.description:
Total number of unavailable pods targeted by this deployment. This is the total number of
pods that are still required for the deployment to have 100% available capacity. They may
either be pods that are running but not yet available or pods that still have not been created.
+optional

updatedReplicas.description:
Total number of non-terminated pods targeted by this deployment that have the desired template spec.
+optional

archived.description: Archived is set to true once approval is finally approved/rejected

digest.description:
Digest is used to verify that images are the ones that got the approvals.
If digest doesn't match for the image, votes are reset.

identifier.description:
Identifier is used to inform user about specific
Helm release or k8s deployment
ie: k8s <namespace>/<deployment name>
    helm: <namespace>/<release name>

rejected.description:
Explicitly rejected approval
can be set directly by user
so even if deadline is not reached approval
could be turned down

voters.description:
Voters is a list of voter
IDs for audit

votesRequired.description:
Requirements for the update such as number of votes
and deadline

payload.description: can be used for bigger messages such as webhook payload
